source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The rectangle $A B C D$ has area 36. A circle with center in point $O$ is inscribed in the triangle $A B D$. What is the area of the rectangle $O P C R$?
<image1>
extraction_notes: 题目包含<image1>且点P、R未在文字中定义，明显依赖图像

image_dependency: Question refers to geometric points O, P, C, R whose relationships are not fully specified in text alone
Computing area of rectangle OPCR depends on diagram-based spatial layout inside rectangle ABCD and triangle ABD
multi_step: Problem combines given area of rectangle ABCD with an inscribed circle in triangle ABD and asks for a derived sub-rectangle area
Likely requires identifying geometric relations from the diagram and then using at least one additional area/length inference
No multiple-choice structure; answer must be derived rather than selected
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value: 18
Geometric result is in principle checkable by a downstream solver or symbolic/diagram-based verification process
asset_registry_record shows question and answer are present and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question, answer, and required image asset are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image dependence is acceptable here because the diagram is available
initial scores indicate strong verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value
